expectations.text: Output identifies YEAR(o_orderdate) as non-sargable and explains why it prevents index usage
expectations.evidence: Problem Summary section: 'the predicate non-sargable — MySQL cannot use the index on o_orderdate because the column is hidden inside a function expression.'

expectations.text: Rewritten SQL uses a range condition (>= and <) instead of the YEAR() function
expectations.evidence: Optimized SQL: WHERE o_orderdate >= '1994-01-01' AND o_orderdate < '1995-01-01'

expectations.text: Plan comparison is included with before/after metrics (rows examined, access type, key used)
expectations.evidence: Explain Plan Comparison section with cached evidence showing cost, rows, strategy, and index usage

expectations.text: Output explicitly mentions the index that would be used (idx_orders_date or idx_orders_o_orderdate)
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'idx_orders_date' explicitly and shows it used for index range scan

expectations.text: Output explains that ORDER BY DESC benefits from backward index scan
expectations.evidence: Optimized section: 'Translates ORDER BY o_orderdate DESC into a backward index scan (no filesort)'
